name: Effector (Business Logic Framework)

performance.notes: Event-driven reactive; very efficient update propagation

agent_readiness.notes: Business logic-first state management. createStore(), createEvent(), createEffect(). sample() for combining events/stores. guard(), split() for conditional logic. effector-react, effector-vue, effector-solid bindings. Fork API for SSR (isolated units). Strongly typed. Popular in Russian-speaking dev community. Advanced: combine(), merge(), attach(), scopeBind(). More opinionated than Zustand — designed for complex business logic, not just state storage. effector-patronum utility library.

gotchas: Less popular in Western markets (strong Eastern European community)
Different mental model from React hooks or Zustand
Verbose for simple state management use cases
best_when: Complex business logic with many interdependent state relationships
Apps needing SSR with isolated state per request (Fork API)
Teams preferring explicit event-driven data flow
Large-scale apps where state logic complexity needs clear structure
summary: Effector is a business logic-first state management library using events, stores, and effects. createStore/createEvent/createEffect primitives. sample() for composing data flows. effector-react/vue/solid bindings. Fork API for SSR isolation. Strongly typed. Best for complex business logic with many interdependent state relationships. Steeper learning curve than Zustand but more powerful for domain-heavy applications.
